channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Previous execution reveals how South Carolina will put 2nd inmate to death by firing squad
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina is legally required to keep some protocols secret as it prepares to put a man to death by firing squad for the murder of a public safety officer, but the only previous execution by bullets the state carried out last month reveals a few key details.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Gates reveals how much inheritance he'll leave for kids
item.description: Bill Gates will leave approximately $1 billion to his children, or about 1% of his $102.2 billion fortune.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Day 3: More former students to discuss 'naked fat tests' in Aaron Thomas trial
item.description: A key witness has already testified that the former high school coach became visibly aroused during one of the tests.
item.dc:creator: Eli Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Stocks shake in twitchy trading as bonds show more stress following tariff escalations
item.description: The U.S. stock market is quivering but holding relatively steady in early Wednesday trading after other markets worldwide swung sharply as President Donald Trump’s trade war keeps escalating.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: It's Met Gala time! Get yourself suitable with this guide on what to expect
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As springtime sunshine finally hits New York City, the Met Gala beckons — one of the most anticipated and visible parties in the world. This year, the theme — “Tailored For You" — is focused on classic tailoring.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job: Shopify CEO
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration halts $1 billion in federal funding for Cornell, $790 million for Northwestern
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than $1 billion in federal funding for Cornell University and around $790 million for Northwestern University has been frozen while the government investigates alleged civil rights violations at the schools, the White House said.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, CHEYANNE MUMPHREY and SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump to fine migrants up to $1K daily for defying deportation order
item.description: The administration is planning to retroactively apply the fines for up to five years, which means some immigrants could owe more than $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, KRON4

item.title: Takeaways from AP and Lee's report on a pipeline company's lawsuits against Midwest farmers
item.description: The company behind a massive proposed carbon pipeline in the Midwestern U.S. filed hundreds of lawsuits against landowners in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Eric Ferkenhoff/Lee Enterprises And Josh Kelety/Ap, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A pipeline company filed hundreds of lawsuits against landowners. Now its project is threatened
item.description: The company behind a massive proposed carbon pipeline in the Midwestern U.S. filed hundreds of lawsuits against landowners in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Eric Ferkenhoff/Lee Enterprises And Josh Kelety/Ap, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump botching tariff messaging because he lacks empathy: Cenk Uygur
item.description: "Why is [Trump] blowing it? Partly because he lacks empathy," Uygur told NewsNation's Leland Vittert on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Companies need 'time machine' for tariffs to make sense: Andrew Yang
item.description: "Instead of reshoring manufacturing, [CEOs] are just cutting investment, they're laying people off," Yang said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: Idaho police officers opened fire from behind a chain-link fence just seconds after exiting their patrol cars and critically wounded a teenage boy — described by his family as nonverbal, autistic and intellectually disabled — as he stepped toward them with a knife, video from a witness shows.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, CLAUDIA LAUER and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump admin pauses $1B from Cornell, $790M from Northwestern amid civil rights investigation: report
item.description: The Trump admin is freezing $1 billion in federal funding from Cornell and $790 million from Northwestern University as both face civil rights investigations, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kentucky watches for surging rivers to recede so widespread cleanup can begin
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — After days of deluges overfilled rivers to near-record levels across Kentucky, residents were anxious Tuesday to return to their flooded homes and assess what's salvageable, even as stubbornly high waters kept some of them waiting even longer.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia shooting leaves at least 3 dead, 3 injured
item.description: At least three people are dead and another three people are injured after a shooting Tuesday evening in Spotsylvania County, Virginia, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Youth soccer coach charged in the death of 13-year-old found on Southern California road
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A youth soccer coach has been charged in the death of a 13-year-old whose body was found on the side of a Southern California road.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Surgery nightmare: Hospital admits mistake after failed cancer surgery attempt
item.description: An investigation is underway at MountainView Hospital in Las Vegas after a cancer patient woke up to startling news in the operating room.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Murphy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A mix of clouds and sunshine expected with slightly cooler afternoon temperatures
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) - Happy Tuesday, Utah! A weak front moved through northern Utah Tuesday morning, bringing a few more clouds, breezy conditions, and a slight chance for isolated showers north of I-80.
item.dc:creator: Alana Brophy, ABC 4

item.title: Shake Shack brings Dubai Chocolate Pistachio Shake to select L.A. locations
item.description: For those who were unsuccessful in recreating the viral Dubai chocolate bar or can’t afford a trip to get an authentic bar, Shake Shack has you covered. The fast-casual chain launched its Dubai Chocolate Pistachio Shake on Monday at select locations in New York City, Los Angeles, and Miami.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democratic-led cities and states push back on threats to cut US school funding over DEI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Some Democratic-led states and cities are pushing back on a Trump administration threat to cut education funding over diversity, equity and inclusion programs, creating a standoff that could test how far the White House is willing to go to press its demands on the nation’s schools.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Last chance to claim $1,400 stimulus check from 2021 nears
item.description: Taxpayers who haven’t filed their 2021 tax returns may still be eligible to receive one (or more) stimulus payments sent during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Trump admin to fire some probationary workers
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration Tuesday by enabling officials to fire thousands of federal workers in their probationary period, saying the government employee unions that sued don’t have legal standing.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Stephen A. Smith 'leaving all doors open' for presidential bid
item.description: Smith has a longstanding history of weighing in on politics, but has been particularly critical of the Democratic Party in the wake of President Trump’s success in the 2024 election, pushing for it to refocus on top issues for voters in last year’s election cycle including immigration and inflation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: House Democrats targeting 3 Florida Republicans in 2026 midterms
item.description: Last month, the National Republican Congressional Campaign Committee (NRCC) released its target list, which included 26 House Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Dire wolf puppies howl for the first time in over 10,000 years
item.description: The dire wolf, who has been extinct for over 10,000 years was brought back to life as the world's first de-extinct animal.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: RFK Jr. says he plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — U.S. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Monday said he plans to tell the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to stop recommending fluoridation in communities nationwide.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah Red Cross volunteers deployed to assist with flooding, tornado relief in several other states
item.description: Volunteers with the American Red Cross of Utah disaster team have been deployed to several states that have been affected by recent tornadoes and flooding in the Midwest and South.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Jeffries accuses Trump of tanking the economy to create opportunities for the rich
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Monday accused President Trump of intentionally crashing the economy to benefit the GOP’s wealthy donors and other well-heeled interests.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Reversible damage found in marathon runners' brains: study
item.description: A 26.2-mile run can do some damage to the brain, a study shows, but the impact is temporary and reversible.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, Fox 5

item.title: VIDEO: 6-year-old found wandering alone with dog in Florida; mom, 1 other charged
item.description: The Volusia Sheriff's Office said a 6-year-old girl and her dog were found wandering alone on Friday afternoon in Deltona.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to block order to return Maryland man from El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration on Monday asked the Supreme Court to block a court order requiring it to return a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Miami’s ‘Little Venezuela’ fears Trump's moves against migration
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Wilmer Escaray left Venezuela in 2007 and enrolled at Miami Dade College, opening his first restaurant six years later.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: At least 16 dead in flooding and tornadoes as storms slash from Texas to Ohio
item.description: DYERSBURG, Tennessee. (AP) — Another round of torrential rain and flash flooding came Saturday for parts of the South and Midwest already heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that also spawned deadly tornadoes. Forecasters warned that rivers in some places would continue to rise for days.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and OBED LAMY, Associated Press, Fox 5
